steps.title: Use the first point to find b
steps.content:
Since $(0, 10)$ lies on the graph, $h(0) = 10$. Substituting into $h(x) = a^x + b$:
$$a^0 + b = 10$$
$$1 + b = 10$$
$$b = 9$$

steps.title: Use the second point to find a
Compute ab
steps.content:
Multiply the values we found:
$$ab = 6 \cdot 9 = 54$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
